examples.title: Algorithm: Disjoint Set Array Representation
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating array-based representation of disjoint sets with find and union operations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM DisjointSetOperations
INPUT: Number of elements n
OUTPUT: Operations for disjoint set management

// ==================== INITIALIZATION ====================
ALGORITHM InitializeDisjointSet(n)
INPUT: n - number of elements
OUTPUT: Initialized parent array

BEGIN
 CREATE array parent[0 to n-1]
 FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 parent[i] ← -1 // Each element is its own parent (root)
 END FOR
 RETURN parent
END

// ==================== FIND OPERATION ====================
ALGORITHM FindParent(parent, element)
INPUT: parent array, element
OUTPUT: Root parent of the element

BEGIN
 IF parent[element] = -1 THEN
 RETURN element // Element is root
 ELSE
 RETURN FindParent(parent, parent[element]) // Recursive call
 END IF
END

// ==================== UNION OPERATION ====================
ALGORITHM UnionSets(parent, set1, set2)
INPUT: parent array, two elements to merge
OUTPUT: Updated parent array

BEGIN
 root1 ← FindParent(parent, set1)
 root2 ← FindParent(parent, set2)
 
 IF root1 ≠ root2 THEN
 parent[root1] ← root2 // Make root2 as parent of root1
 END IF
END

// ==================== CHECK IF SAME SET ====================
ALGORITHM AreInSameSet(parent, element1, element2)
INPUT: parent array, two elements
OUTPUT: Boolean indicating if they are in same set

BEGIN
 root1 ← FindParent(parent, element1)
 root2 ← FindParent(parent, element2)
 RETURN (root1 = root2)
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates three fundamental operations on disjoint sets using array representation: 1) Initialize creates n separate sets with each element as its own parent. 2) FindParent uses recursion to trace up to the root element. 3) Union merges two sets by making one root the parent of another. These operations form the basis of the union-find data structure.

examples.title: Python: Disjoint Set Array Implementation
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of disjoint set data structure with path compression and union by rank
examples.code:
class DisjointSet:
 """
 Disjoint Set (Union-Find) Data Structure using Array Representation
 Implements path compression and union by rank for optimization
 """
 
 def __init__(self, n):
 """Initialize n separate sets, each element is its own parent"""
 self.parent = [-1] * n # -1 indicates root (self-parent)
 self.rank = [0] * n # Track tree depth for union by rank
 
 def find_parent(self, element):
 """
 Find the root parent of an element with path compression
 Time Complexity: O(α(n)) - amortized nearly constant
 """
 if self.parent[element] == -1:
 return element # Element is its own parent (root)
 
 # Path compression: directly connect to root
 self.parent[element] = self.find_parent(self.parent[element])
 return self.parent[element]
 
 def union(self, element1, element2):
 """
 Union two sets by rank
 Time Complexity: O(α(n)) - amortized nearly constant
 """
 root1 = self.find_parent(element1)
 root2 = self.find_parent(element2)
 
 if root1 != root2:
 # Union by rank: attach smaller tree under larger tree
 if self.rank[root1] < self.rank[root2]:
 self.parent[root1] = root2
 elif self.rank[root1] > self.rank[root2]:
 self.parent[root2] = root1
 else:
 self.parent[root2] = root1
 self.rank[root1] += 1
 
 def find(self, element1, element2):
 """Check if two elements are in the same set"""
 return self.find_parent(element1) == self.find_parent(element2)
 
 def display(self):
 """Display the current state of the disjoint set"""
 print("Element:", list(range(len(self.parent))))
 print("Parent:", self.parent)
 print("Rank: ", self.rank)


# Demonstration of Disjoint Set operations
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create disjoint set with 5 elements
 ds = DisjointSet(5)
 
 print("=== Initial State ===")
 ds.display()
 
 print("\n=== After Union(0, 1) ===")
 ds.union(0, 1)
 ds.display()
 
 print("\n=== After Union(2, 3) ===")
 ds.union(2, 3)
 ds.display()
 
 print("\n=== After Union(0, 2) ===")
 ds.union(0, 2) # Connect sets {0,1} and {2,3}
 ds.display()
 
 print("\n=== Find Operations ===")
 print(f"Find(0): {ds.find_parent(0)}")
 print(f"Find(3): {ds.find_parent(3)}")
 print(f"Find(4): {ds.find_parent(4)}")
 print(f"Same set (0,3)? {ds.find(0, 3)}")
 print(f"Same set (0,4)? {ds.find(0, 4)}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation includes key optimizations: path compression flattens the tree structure for faster future finds, while union by rank ensures balanced trees. The class provides find_parent() to locate root elements, union() to merge sets, and find() to check set membership. The display method shows how parent array values change as sets are merged.
examples.timeComplexity: O(α(n)) amortized for find and union with optimizations

examples.title: C: Disjoint Set Array Implementation
examples.description: Complete C implementation of disjoint set with find, union, and initialization functions
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates the array representation of disjoint sets. The parent array uses -1 to indicate root elements (each element is initially its own parent). The findParent() function uses recursion with path compression to efficiently find root elements. The unionSets() function implements union by rank to keep trees balanced. The implementation includes initialization, find, union, and same-set checking operations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(α(n)) amortized with path compression and union by rank

examples.title: Python: Linked List Representation
examples.description: Alternative Python implementation using linked list representation for educational comparison
examples.code:
class Node:
 """Node for linked list representation of disjoint sets"""
 def __init__(self, data):
 self.data = data
 self.next = None


class LinkedListSet:
 """
 Disjoint Set using Linked List Representation
 Each set is represented as a linked list
 """
 
 def __init__(self):
 self.head = None
 self.size = 0
 
 def add_element(self, data):
 """
 Add an element to the set
 Time Complexity: O(n) - must traverse to end
 """
 new_node = Node(data)
 
 if self.head is None:
 self.head = new_node
 else:
 current = self.head
 while current.next is not None:
 current = current.next
 current.next = new_node
 
 self.size += 1
 
 def contains(self, data):
 """Check if element exists in set"""
 current = self.head
 while current is not None:
 if current.data == data:
 return True
 current = current.next
 return False
 
 def display(self):
 """Display all elements in the set"""
 elements = []
 current = self.head
 while current is not None:
 elements.append(str(current.data))
 current = current.next
 print(" -> ".join(elements) if elements else "(empty)")


class DisjointSetLinkedList:
 """
 Collection of disjoint sets using linked list representation
 Each set is a separate linked list
 """
 
 def __init__(self, num_elements):
 # Create individual sets for each element
 self.sets = []
 for i in range(num_elements):
 new_set = LinkedListSet()
 new_set.add_element(i)
 self.sets.append(new_set)
 
 def find_set(self, element):
 """Find which set contains the element"""
 for idx, s in enumerate(self.sets):
 if s.contains(element):
 return idx
 return -1
 
 def union(self, element1, element2):
 """Merge sets containing element1 and element2"""
 set1_idx = self.find_set(element1)
 set2_idx = self.find_set(element2)
 
 # If in same set or invalid, do nothing
 if set1_idx == -1 or set2_idx == -1 or set1_idx == set2_idx:
 return
 
 # Merge set2 into set1 (append all elements)
 set1 = self.sets[set1_idx]
 set2 = self.sets[set2_idx]
 
 # Find end of set1 and link to set2
 current = set1.head
 while current.next is not None:
 current = current.next
 current.next = set2.head
 
 # Update size
 set1.size += set2.size
 
 # Remove set2 from collection
 self.sets.pop(set2_idx)
 
 def display_all_sets(self):
 """Display all disjoint sets"""
 for idx, s in enumerate(self.sets):
 print(f"Set {idx}:", end=" ")
 s.display()


# Demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
 print("=== Linked List Representation Demo ===\n")
 
 # Create 5 elements, each in its own set
 ds = DisjointSetLinkedList(5)
 
 print("Initial sets:")
 ds.display_all_sets()
 
 print("\n=== After Union(0, 1) ===")
 ds.union(0, 1)
 ds.display_all_sets()
 
 print("\n=== After Union(2, 3) ===")
 ds.union(2, 3)
 ds.display_all_sets()
 
 print("\n=== After Union(0, 2) ===")
 ds.union(0, 2)
 ds.display_all_sets()
 
 print("\nFind set containing element 3:", ds.find_set(3))
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the linked list representation of disjoint sets as an alternative to array representation. Each set is stored as a separate linked list, and the union operation merges two lists by appending one to another. This representation is simpler conceptually but less efficient for find operations, requiring O(n) to add elements and search. The code serves as an educational comparison to the array-based approach.
